fields.description:
## Why
Audit Maintainability finding #3 (MEDIUM). The codebase reads ~80 distinct `REBAR_*` env vars across ~133 `os.environ`/`getenv` sites; only ~42 sit in the three intended config modules (config.py, llm/config.py, review_bot/config.py) — the rest are scattered (worst in the reconciler). There is no single artifact enumerating what can be set, so discovering the config surface needs a grep and a new env var can ship undocumented. The repo already solved this exact shape twice (the derived prompt-index `reviewers/index.json` and generated TypedDicts, both with regenerate-and-diff CI drift gates).

## What / files
- `scripts/gen_env_registry.py`: scan `src/rebar/**/*.py` for `REBAR_[A-Z0-9_]+` referenced via `os.environ[...]` / `os.environ.get(...)` / `os.getenv(...)` (AST or regex over reads), and emit a canonical, deterministically-sorted registry table to `docs/env-vars.md`: columns = var name, reading module(s) (relative path), deprecated-alias status. Mark the known alias pairs (`REBAR_PUSH` → `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH`; `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` → `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`). Support a `--check` mode that regenerates in-memory and exits nonzero on any diff vs the committed file (mirroring the prompt-index generator's `--check`).
- `docs/env-vars.md`: the committed generated artifact (header note: "generated by scripts/gen_env_registry.py — do not edit by hand; run `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py`").
- `.github/workflows/test.yml`: add an "Env-var registry drift gate" step modeled EXACTLY on the existing "Prompt-index drift gate" (~L224-240) — run the generator, then `git diff --exit-code -- docs/env-vars.md` (or the generator's `--check`), failing the build on drift with a message telling the dev to regenerate.
- A pointer from README/docs (e.g. docs/README.md index or config.md) to docs/env-vars.md.

## Scope
In: the generator script, the committed registry doc, the CI drift-gate step, a docs pointer, and one-line doc-comment additions for any undocumented var the generator surfaces. Out: consolidating the scattered `os.environ` reads into the config modules (a separate follow-on refactor); adding or removing any env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/env-vars.md` is committed and lists every `REBAR_*` var read anywhere under `src/rebar`, with reading module(s) and deprecation status; regenerating it produces no diff
- [ ] `scripts/gen_env_registry.py --check` exits 0 on a clean tree and nonzero when a new `REBAR_*` read is added without regenerating (covered by a unit test on the generator, or demonstrated in the gate)
- [ ] The generator marks the known deprecated aliases (REBAR_PUSH, REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE)
- [ ] A drift-gate step exists in test.yml modeled on the prompt-index gate; adding a `REBAR_*` read without regenerating fails CI
- [ ] README/docs point to docs/env-vars.md; `make lint` green
